finding: Replacement of the aminoisoquinoline back-pocket group with a smaller aminopyridine bearing a methyl group reduced aromatic ring count while maintaining occupancy of the lipophilic cavity and enforcing a desired dihedral angle about the biaryl bond.
structural_feature: aminopyridine with methyl group replacing aminoisoquinoline at back-pocket position
evidence_quote: To reduce aromatic ring count, the aminoisoquinoline was replaced with an aminopyridine featuring a methyl group, which was designed to fill the lipophilic cavity of this region while enforcing a desired dihedral angle about the biaryl bond.

finding: Optimization of the back-pocket region by filling the lipophilic cavity between the alpha-2 (switch-II) helix and the alpha-3 helix enhanced potency, with Asp 69 serving as a hydrogen bond acceptor.
structural_feature: aromatic groups in the back-pocket region
evidence_quote: Previous KRAS G12C inhibitor studies suggested that the switch-II helix could potentially rotate to accommodate aromatic groups in the back-pocket and that Asp 69 was positioned to serve as a hydrogen bond acceptor.

finding: Divarasib has a significant noncovalent binding component that contributes to its high potency and rapid alkylation kinetics compared with other KRAS G12C inhibitors.
evidence_quote: We demonstrate a significant noncovalent binding component of divarasib that contributes to its potency and rapid kinetics.

finding: Improvements in noncovalent Ki drove improvements in both potency and kinetics of covalent alkylation, consistent with a two-step kinetic model for covalent inhibition.
evidence_quote: While our data and analysis strongly suggest that improvements in noncovalent K

finding: A correlation was observed between the biochemical SOS exchange assay potency and cellular target engagement across the compound series.

finding: Divarasib demonstrated greater potency and faster kinetics of alkylation compared with other KRAS G12C inhibitors in vitro.
structural_feature: divarasib scaffold vs other KRAS G12C inhibitors
evidence_quote: Divarasib has greater potency and kinetics of alkylation compared with other KRAS G12C inhibitors in vitro

finding: An isoquinoline moiety attached at quinazoline-C7 was used as initial attempt to fill the lipophilic back-pocket cavity.
evidence_quote: In an attempt to fill the lipophilic cavity of the back-pocket region, an isoquinoline moiety was attached at quinazoline-C7 1

finding: Non-KRAS G12C driven cell lines showed lack of sensitivity to divarasib, demonstrating high selectivity for KRAS G12C mutant cells.
evidence_quote: The lack of sensitivity of non-KRAS G12C driven cell lines left us with an incomplete picture of cellular selectivity.
